agency: Manhattan District Attorney's Office (New York County District Attorney)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA; manhattanda.org served no robots.txt). Fingerprinted the site as WordPress (custom 'dany' theme) behind the Sucuri CloudProxy WAF/CDN, with Jetpack Search, The Events Calendar, WPForms / Contact Form 7, Mailchimp, Google reCAPTCHA, and an exposed WP REST API (/wp-json, 424 routes; wp/v2/posts reports x-wp-total: 3029). Verified via the Socrata Discovery API that NO NYC Open Data asset exists under any Manhattan-DA agency label (four exact-label queries, all zero; keyword q=district attorney / q=prosecution return only other jurisdictions and other NYC agencies). Read the Contact page FOIL section, which delegates all records requests to NYC OpenRecords.
headline_findings: The Manhattan DA publishes NO NYC Open Data — as an independently elected county prosecutor it sits outside the mayoral open-data program; four agency-label queries against the Socrata Discovery API return zero datasets.
The only genuinely machine-readable object is accidental: the newsroom (~3,029 posts) is JSON solely because WordPress ships a REST API (wp/v2/posts). It is undocumented, unversioned, content-only.

Nothing the office actually does has an API — prosecutions, bureaus/initiatives, victim services, and tips exist only as HTML pages or prose; reporting suspected wrongdoing means a generic reCAPTCHA contact form or a phone call.
Two core workflows are outsourced or un-built: FOIL records requests are delegated off-site to NYC OpenRecords, and tip intake has no structured surface at all.

All five NYC District Attorneys run the same functions on the same kind of stack — this is a candidate for ONE shared DA API rather than five silos. Net-new write surface: submit a tip (TipSubmission). See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

meta.platform: WordPress (custom 'dany' theme) behind Sucuri CloudProxy; Jetpack Search; The Events Calendar; WPForms/Contact Form 7; Mailchimp; exposed WP REST API; FOIL delegated to NYC OpenRecords
meta.netnew: TipSubmission — submit a routable tip to a DA bureau (no API today; only a generic reCAPTCHA contact form). FOIL is delegated off-site to NYC OpenRecords.
meta.tagline: A WordPress newsroom whose only API is accidental, no open data at all, FOIL delegated and tips un-built — five identical DA offices waiting on one shared contract.
meta.headline: The Manhattan DA has essentially no structured surface: its lone machine-readable object is an accidental WordPress feed, so the work is to define contracts once and standardize them across all five borough DAs.

scorecard_notes.platform: modern WordPress CMS, but heavily rented (Sucuri WAF, Jetpack, WordPress.com search)
scorecard_notes.siteApi: a real but accidental WP REST API over the newsroom; undocumented, unversioned, content-only
scorecard_notes.openData: zero NYC Open Data assets — verified across four agency-label queries
scorecard_notes.coreData: only the newsroom is structured; prosecutions, programs, and services are HTML/prose, no case data
scorecard_notes.writeApi: no tip-intake API; only a generic reCAPTCHA contact form, and FOIL delegated off-site
scorecard_notes.agent: no MCP or agent-native surface exists today
scorecard_notes.ownership: content self-hosted, but search rented (Jetpack), FOIL delegated (OpenRecords), security/CDN rented (Sucuri)

apis_observed.note: The one real API. wp/v2/posts returns the newsroom as JSON, x-wp-total: 3029 (press releases, media coverage, op-eds, reports, testimony). Accidental (WP default), undocumented, content-only.
Community events feed via the Modern Tribe Events Calendar plugin.
On-site search delegated to Jetpack Search; not owned by the office.
Generic contact form is the closest thing to tip intake — no schema, no structured API.
The office delegates all FOIL requests here; its statutory intake workflow lives off its own domain. No DA-owned API.
server: Sucuri/Cloudproxy fronts the whole site.
reCAPTCHA gates forms; exactmetrics/v1 and wordfence/v1 REST namespaces present.

fruit.name: Newsroom (press releases, media coverage, op-eds, reports)
fruit.notes: The one genuinely machine-readable object — wp/v2/posts, x-wp-total: 3029, across categories press-release (915), media-coverage (1963), op-ed, reports, testimony, remarks, video, indictments. Accidental (WP default), undocumented, content-only.

fruit.name: Our Work — bureaus, units & initiatives
fruit.notes: CPU (Community Partnerships), gun violence, hate crimes, white-collar crime, reform & fairness, holding the system accountable, sexual & gender-based violence — plus diversion and post-conviction / conviction-integrity work. HTML pages only; no structured API.

fruit.name: Victim Resources
fruit.notes: Resources by crime type: child abuse, elder abuse, financial crimes, frauds targeting immigrants, hate crimes, housing/tenant protection, human trafficking, intimate partner & sexual violence, worker protection / wage theft. HTML only.

fruit.name: Office locations & contact
fruit.notes: Main Office (One Hogan Place, 212.335.9000), Harlem Office (163 West 125th Street), Washington Heights Office (530 West 166th Street). Published as HTML on the Contact page.

fruit.name: Prosecution / caseload statistics (aggregate)
fruit.notes: Arraignments, indictments, dispositions, convictions, declinations, diversions — the office's core subject, published only as prose in press releases and periodic reports. No NYC Open Data twin; aggregate only, never an individual case.

fruit.name: Community events
fruit.notes: Community events exposed by the Modern Tribe Events Calendar plugin REST API — a secondary machine-readable surface.

fruit.name: FOIL records request
fruit.notes: The Contact page states all FOIL requests must be submitted via NYC OpenRecords (select 'Manhattan District Attorney's Office'), with a U.S.-mail fallback to the Records Access Officer at One Hogan Place. Delegated off the DA's own domain; no DA-owned API. An alternative net-new write surface.

fruit.name: Report a tip to a bureau
fruit.notes: Reporting suspected wrongdoing (financial fraud, elder abuse, human trafficking, hate crime, public corruption, cybercrime) has no API — only a generic reCAPTCHA-gated Contact Form 7 form or a bureau phone line. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.
